revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:November (旧暦)]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of November (旧暦)]]</includeonly>

'''旧暦11月'''は、[[旧暦]]（[[太陰太陽暦]]）の年初から11番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[冬至]]を含む月が11月となる。[[新暦]]では[[11月]]下旬から[[1月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
11月の別名は'''霜月'''（'''しもつき'''）である。名前の由来は[[11月]]を参照のこと。
11月30日は存在しない年もある
== 関連項目 == {{月 (暦)|旧暦11月|旧暦360日|月 (旧暦)|11|日 (旧暦)|[[11月30日 (旧暦)|30]]}}

revision.comment: 30日の存在についての記述を、[[5月 (旧暦)]]より転載
revision.text:
'''旧暦11月'''（きゅうれきじゅういちがつ）は、[[旧暦]]（[[太陰太陽暦]]）の年初から11番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[冬至]]を含む月が11月となる。[[新暦]]では[[11月]]下旬から[[1月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
11月の別名は'''霜月'''（'''しもつき'''）である。名前の由来は[[11月]]を参照のこと。
東洋の太陰太陽暦では月の日数である[[大小]]（大月30日、小月29日）が年により異なるため、11月29日までで11月30日は存在しない年もある。
== 関連項目 == {{月 (暦)|旧暦11月|旧暦360日|月 (旧暦)|11|日 (旧暦)|[[11月30日 (旧暦)|30]]}}

'''Lunar November'''（きゅうれきじゅういちがつ）は、[[旧暦]]（[[太陰太陽暦]]）の年初から11番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[冬至]]を含む月が11月となる。[[新暦]]では[[11月]]下旬から[[1月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
11月の別名は'''霜月'''（しもつき）である。名前の由来は[[11月]]を参照のこと。異名は「ちゅうとう（仲冬）」。
東洋の太陰太陽暦では月の日数である[[大小]]（大月30日、小月29日）が年により異なるため、11月29日までで11月30日は存在しない年もある。

'''Lunar November'''（きゅうれきじゅういちがつ）は、[[旧暦]]（[[太陰太陽暦]]）の年初から11番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[冬至]]を含む月が11月となる。[[新暦]]では[[11月]]下旬から[[1月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
11月の別名は'''霜月'''（しもつき）である。名前の由来は[[11月]]を参照のこと。異名は「ちゅうとう（仲冬）」。
東洋の太陰太陽暦では月の日数である[[大小]]（大月30日、小月29日）が年により異なるため、11月29日までで11月30日は存在しない年もある。

revision.text:
{{Lunar November}} '''Lunar November'''（きゅうれきじゅういちがつ）は、[[旧暦]]（[[太陰太陽暦]]）の年初から11番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[冬至]]を含む月が11月となる。[[新暦]]では[[11月]]下旬から[[1月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
11月の別名は'''霜月'''（しもつき）である。名前の由来は[[11月]]を参照のこと。異名は「ちゅうとう（仲冬）」。
東洋の太陰太陽暦では月の日数である[[大小]]（大月30日、小月29日）が年により異なるため、11月29日までで11月30日は存在しない年もある。

== 関連項目 == {{月 (暦)|Lunar November|旧暦の日付の一覧|月 (旧暦)|11|日 (旧暦)|[[11月30日 (旧暦)|30]]}} [[Category:Lunar November|*]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Japanese Lunar Calendar Months|11]][[Category:qq|*]] [[Category:kk]][[en:11月 (旧暦)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Japanese Lunar Calendar Months|11]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[Category:qq]] {{citation needed}}
{{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk hak]] [[gan:十一月]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:11月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월]] [[th:เดือน 11]] [[vi:Tháng mười một]] [[wuu:农历十一月]] [[za:Ndwensapyat]] [[zh_min_nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]] [[zh_yue:十一月]] [[zh:农历十一月]]

{{Lunar November}} '''Lunar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月}} of the year in the {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦}} ({{ill|Lunisolar Calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|ja|11月}} to early {{ill|January|ja|1月}}.
Another name for November is {{reading|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|November|ja|11月}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
== Shimozuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Shimozuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! {{Lunar November}} '''Lunar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月}} of the year in the {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|ja|11月}} to early {{ill|January|ja|1月}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|November|ja|11月}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.

{{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk hak]] [[gan:十一月]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:11月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 11월]] [[th:เดือน 11]] [[vi:Tháng mười một]] [[wuu:农历十一月]] [[za:Ndwensapyat]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十一月]] [[zh:农历十一月]]

{{Lunar November}} '''Lunar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月}} of the year in the {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|ja|11月}} to early {{ill|January|ja|1月}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|November|ja|11月}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.

{{Lunar November}} '''Lunar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月}} of the year in the {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|ja|11月}} to early {{ill|January|ja|1月}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|November|ja|11月}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.

revision.text:
{{Lunar November}} '''Lunar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|ja|11月}} to early {{ill|January|ja|1月}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|November|ja|11月}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
== Shimozuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Shimozuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! {{Lunar November}} '''Lunar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|ja|11月}} to early {{ill|January|ja|1月}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|November|ja|11月}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.

{{Lunar November}} '''Lunar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|ja|11月}} to early {{ill|January|ja|1月}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|November|ja|11月}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.

{{Lunisolar November}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|ja|11月}} to early {{ill|January|ja|1月}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|November|ja|11月}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.

revision.text:
{{Lunisolar November}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月|12=draft}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至|12=draft}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|ja|11月|12=draft}} to early {{ill|January|ja|1月|12=draft}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|November|ja|11月|12=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
== Shimozuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Shimozuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q905859]] Wikidata name: 11th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
The month that includes the [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]] becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:November|November]] to early [[:en:January|January]].
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:11月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|qq=|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same [[:en:Day of the week|day of the week]] as [[:en:March|March]] of that year, and in common years also the same as [[:en:February|February]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh_min_nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q905859]] Wikidata name: 11th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
The month that includes the [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]] becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:November|November]] to early [[:en:January|January]].
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.

Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same [[:en:Day of the week|day of the week]] as [[:en:March|March]] of that year, and in common years also the same as [[:en:February|February]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q905859]] Wikidata name: 11th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
The month that includes the [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]] becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:November|November]] to early [[:en:January|January]].
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:11月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|qq=|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same [[:en:Day of the week|day of the week]] as [[:en:March|March]] of that year, and in common years also the same as [[:en:February|February]].

[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk hak]] [[gan:十一月]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍tja]] [[wuu:农历十一月]] [[de:November (Lunisolar)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q905859]] Wikidata name: 11th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
The month that includes the [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]] becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:November|November]] to early [[:en:January|January]].
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:11月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same [[:en:Day of the week|day of the week]] as [[:en:March|March]] of that year, and in common years also the same as [[:en:February|February]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q905859]] Wikidata name: 11th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
The month that includes the [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]] becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:November|November]] to early [[:en:January|January]].
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:11月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same [[:en:Day of the week|day of the week]] as [[:en:March|March]] of that year, and in common years also the same as [[:en:February|February]].
== Shimozuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Shimozuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q905859]] Wikidata name: 11th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
The month that includes the [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]] becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:November|November]] to early [[:en:January|January]].
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:11月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same [[:en:Day of the week|day of the week]] as [[:en:March|March]] of that year, and in common years also the same as [[:en:February|February]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q905859]] Wikidata name: 11th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
The month that includes the [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]] becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:November|November]] to early [[:en:January|January]].
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:11月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same [[:en:Day of the week|day of the week]] as [[:en:March|March]] of that year, and in common years also the same as [[:en:February|February]].
== Shimozuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Shimozuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || [[:en:December 22|December 22]] || 2015th year [[:en:January 19|January 19]] || 29 days |- | 2015 || [[:en:December 11|December 11]] || 2016th year [[:en:January 9|January 9]] || 30 days |- | 2016 || [[:en:November 29|November 29]] || [[:en:December 28|December 28]] || 30 days |- | 2017 || [[:en:December 18|December 18]] || 2018th year [[:en:January 16|January 16]] || 30 days |- | 2018 || [[:en:December 7|December 7]] || 2019th year [[:en:January 5|January 5]] || 30 days |- | 2019 || [[:en:November 27|November 27]] || [[:en:December 25|December 25]] || 29 days |- | 2020 || [[:en:December 15|December 15]] || 2021st year [[:en:January 12|January 12]] || 29 days |- | 2021 || [[:en:December 4|December 4]] || 2022nd year [[:en:January 2|January 2]] || 30 days |- | 2022 || [[:en:November 24|November 24]] || [[:en:December 22|December 22]] || 29 days |- | 2023 || [[:en:December 13|December 13]] || 2024th year [[:en:January 10|January 10]] || 29 days |- | 2024 || [[:en:December 1|December 1]] || [[:en:December 30|December 30]] || 30 days |- | 2025 || [[:en:December 20|December 20]] || 2026th year [[:en:January 18|January 18]] || 30 days |}

In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:November|November]] to early [[:en:January|January]].
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh_min_nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh_min_nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q905859]] Wikidata name: 11th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
The month that includes the [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]] becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:November|November]] to early [[:en:January|January]].
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:11月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same [[:en:Day of the week|day of the week]] as [[:en:March|March]] of that year, and in common years also the same as [[:en:February|February]].

Number of Days |- | 2014 || [[:en:December 22|December 22]] || 2015th year [[:en:January 19|January 19]] || 29 days |- | 2015 || [[:en:December 11|December 11]] || 2016th year [[:en:January 9|January 9]] || 30 days |- | 2016 || [[:en:November 29|November 29]] || [[:en:December 28|December 28]] || 30 days |- | 2017 || [[:en:December 18|December 18]] || 2018th year [[:en:January 16|January 16]] || 30 days |- | 2018 || [[:en:December 7|December 7]] || 2019th year [[:en:January 5|January 5]] || 30 days |- | 2019 || [[:en:November 27|November 27]] || [[:en:December 25|December 25]] || 29 days |- | 2020 || [[:en:December 15|December 15]] || 2021st year [[:en:January 12|January 12]] || 29 days |- | 2021 || [[:en:December 4|December 4]] || 2022nd year [[:en:January 2|January 2]] || 30 days |- | 2022 || [[:en:November 24|November 24]] || [[:en:December 22|December 22]] || 29 days |- | 2023 || [[:en:December 13|December 13]] || 2024th year [[:en:January 10|January 10]] || 29 days |- | 2024 || [[:en:December 1|December 1]] || [[:en:December 30|December 30]] || 30 days |- | 2025 || [[:en:December 20|December 20]] || 2026th year [[:en:January 18|January 18]] || 30 days |}

[[ko:음력 11월]] [[th:เดือน 11]] [[vi:Tháng mười một]] [[za:Ndwensapyat]] [[zh:农历十一月]] [[ar:November (Lunisolar)]] <references group="lower-alpha"/> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]] [[vi:Tháng Tý]] [[za:Ndwen'it]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十一月]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]] [[vi:Tháng Tý]] [[za:Ndwen'it]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十一月]] {{wikidata link|Q905859}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Lunisolar months]] [[Category:Lunisolar November]] [[Category:Lunisolar November|*]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q905859]] Wikidata name: 11th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
The month that includes the [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]] becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:November|November]] to early [[:en:January|January]].
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:11月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same [[:en:Day of the week|day of the week]] as [[:en:March|March]] of that year, and in common years also the same as [[:en:February|February]].

==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:11月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q905859]] Wikidata name: 11th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|WD=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November}} to early {{ill|January|en|January|WD=Q108|lt=January}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:11月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language}}, November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language}}: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same {{ill|Day of the week|en|Day of the week|WD=Q41825|lt=day of the week}} as {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}} of that year, and in common years also the same as {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q905859]] Wikidata name: 11th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|WD=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November}} to early {{ill|January|en|January|WD=Q108|lt=January}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:11月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language}}, November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language}}: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same {{ill|Day of the week|en|Day of the week|WD=Q41825|lt=day of the week}} as {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}} of that year, and in common years also the same as {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}}.
== Shimozuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Shimozuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|WD=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November}} to early {{ill|January|en|January|WD=Q108|lt=January}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language}}, November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language}}: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same {{ill|Day of the week|en|Day of the week|WD=Q41825|lt=day of the week}} as {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}} of that year, and in common years also the same as {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]]
{{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|WD=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November}} to early {{ill|January|en|January|WD=Q108|lt=January}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Shimotsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language}}, November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language}}: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same {{ill|Day of the week|en|Day of the week|WD=Q41825|lt=day of the week}} as {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}} of that year, and in common years also the same as {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}}.
== Shimozuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Shimozuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || {{ill|December 22|en|December 22|WD=Q2636|lt=December 22}} || 2015th year {{ill|January 19|en|January 19|WD=Q2266|lt=January 19}} || 29 days |- | 2015 || {{ill|December 11|en|December 11|WD=Q2363|lt=December 11}} || 2016th year {{ill|January 9|en|January 9|WD=Q2238|lt=January 9}} || 30 days |- | 2016 || {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29}} || {{ill|December 28|en|December 28|WD=Q2832|lt=December 28}} || 30 days |- | 2017 || {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18}} || 2018th year {{ill|January 16|en|January 16|WD=Q2261|lt=January 16}} || 30 days |- | 2018 || {{ill|December 7|en|December 7|WD=Q2299|lt=December 7}} || 2019th year {{ill|January 5|en|January 5|WD=Q2202|lt=January 5}} || 30 days |- | 2019 || {{ill|November 27|en|November 27|WD=Q3018|lt=November 27}} || {{ill|December 25|en|December 25|WD=Q2745|lt=December 25}} || 29 days |- | 2020 || {{ill|December 15|en|December 15|WD=Q2383|lt=December 15}} || 2021st year {{ill|January 12|en|January 12|WD=Q2248|lt=January 12}} || 29 days |- | 2021 || {{ill|December 4|en|December 4|WD=Q2310|lt=December 4}} || 2022nd year {{ill|January 2|en|January 2|WD=Q2151|lt=January 2}} || 30 days |- | 2022 || {{ill|November 24|en|November 24|WD=Q3022|lt=November 24}} || {{ill|December 22|en|December 22|WD=Q2636|lt=December 22}} || 29 days |- | 2023 || {{ill|December 13|en|December 13|WD=Q2379|lt=December 13}} || 2024th year {{ill|January 10|en|January 10|WD=Q2243|lt=January 10}} || 29 days |- | 2024 || {{ill|December 1|en|December 1|WD=Q2297|lt=December 1}} || {{ill|December 30|en|December 30|WD=Q2901|lt=December 30}} || 30 days |- | 2025 || {{ill|December 20|en|December 20|WD=Q2455|lt=December 20}} || 2026th year {{ill|January 18|en|January 18|WD=Q2265|lt=January 18}} || 30 days |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]]
{{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month|1=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|WD=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice|1=Winter solstice}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=The new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November|1=November}} to early {{ill|January|en|January|WD=Q108|lt=January|1=January}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=The new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Q113497878|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same {{ill|Day of the week|en|Day of the week|WD=Q41825|lt=day of the week|1=Day of the week}} as {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} of that year, and in common years also the same as {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February|1=February}}.

{{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month|1=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}).

In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=The new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Q113497878|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same {{ill|Day of the week|en|Day of the week|WD=Q41825|lt=day of the week|1=Day of the week}} as {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} of that year, and in common years also the same as {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February|1=February}}.
