<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 11 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 11-ngie̍t]][[ja:11月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월]][[th:เดือน 11]][[vi:Tháng Tý]][[za:Ndwen'it]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 11 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十一月]][[zh:农历十一月]]
{{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''Lunisolar November''' (旧暦十一月) is the eleventh {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month|1=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|WD=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice|1=Winter solstice}} becomes November. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=The new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November|1=November}} to early {{ill|January|en|January|WD=Q108|lt=January|1=January}}.
Another name for November is {{nihongo|'''Shimozuki'''|しもつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “Chūtō (仲冬)”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when November ends on the 29th and no November 30th exists.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, the {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar November|lt_ja=旧暦11月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Shimotsuki''' (しもつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for November of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=The new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. {{ill|Shimotsuki|ja|霜月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuki|1=Q113497878|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497878}} literally means “frost month,” when frost descends. Other theories hold that it is an abbreviation of “Oshimono-zuki” (食物月; おしものづき), “food month”; or a corruption of “Shibomutsuki” (凋む月; しぼむつき) or “Suetsutsuki” (末つ月; すえつつき). Alternate names include “Kagurazuki” (神楽月; かぐらづき) and “Nezuki” (子月; ねづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, November is called “Nitek Rankechup” (ニテㇰランケチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: nitek ranke cup), meaning “month of dropping tree branches.”<ref>{{Cite web |author=高橋 靖以 |url=https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |title=月刊シロㇿ 10月号（2015.10） |website=ainugo.nam.go.jp |publisher=[[ウポポイ|国立アイヌ民俗博物館]] |language=ja |date=2015-10 |accessdate=2025-03-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2025-0302-0131-45/https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html |archivedate=2025-03-02 |url-status=live}}</ref>
In English, the month name {{lang|en|November}} derives from the Latin {{lang|la|novem}}, meaning “nine.” The numerical mismatch arises because the pre-46 BCE Roman calendar began in March, making November the ninth month.
November begins on the same {{ill|Day of the week|en|Day of the week|WD=Q41825|lt=day of the week|1=Day of the week}} as {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} of that year, and in common years also the same as {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February|1=February}}.
